Command: read README.md

```text
# NEXCODE-KIBOKO

Local-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.

## What You Get

- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.
- Copilot-style sidebar UX with session history, per-session model/provider/mode controls, and settings drawer.
- Provider connection badge with live health checks and latency display.
- Automatic model suggestions from Ollama/OpenAI-compatible `/models` endpoints.
- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.
- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.
- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.
- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.

## Repository Layout

- `extension/`: VS Code extension.
- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.
- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.
- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.
- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).
- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js `>=18`
- npm `>=9`
- VS Code `>=1.95`
- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.

## Quick Start

1. Install dependencies:
   - `npm install`
2. Build all packages:
   - `npm run build`
3. Optional model pull for local inference:
   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\tools\setup-ollama.ps1`
4. Launch extension host:
   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.
   - Press `F5`.
5. Open the sidebar:
   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.

## Recommended Ollama Model

The sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:

- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`

You can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.

## Chat Command Surface

- Standard prompt:
  - `Build auth middleware with tests`
- Local code search:
  - `/tool search orchestrator`
- Online search (Tavily + fallback):
  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`
- Terminal execution:
  - `/tool terminal npm run test`
- Git and tests:
  - `/tool git-status`
  - `/tool git-diff`
  - `/tool git-branch`
  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`
- Read file:
  - `/tool read README.md`
- Propose code edit:
  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`

## Approval And Safety Flow

- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.
- Every proposal supports:
  - `Preview Diff`
  - `Apply Edit`
  - `Reject`
- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.
- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.

## Extension Settings

All settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:

- `defaultProvider`
- `defaultModel`
- `defaultMode`
- `ollamaBaseUrl`
- `openAIBaseUrl`
- `openAIApiKey`
- `tavilyApiKey`
- `allowToolCommands`
- `requireTerminalApproval`

## Build, Test, Package

- Build:
  - `npm run build`
- Type/lint checks:
  - `npm run lint`
- Tests:
  - `npm run test`
- Full model and tools validation matrix:
  - `npm run validate:models`
- Raw VSIX package:
  - `npm run package:vsix`

## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump

Use the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:

- Package and install:
  - `npm run extension:release`
- Package only:
  - `npm run extension:package`
- Bump only:
  - `npm run extension:bump`

You can choose version increment type:

- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`
- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`

## Maintenance And Cleanup

- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:
  - `npm run clean`
- Run full local checks:
  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\tools\run-local-checks.ps1`

Ignored/generated content includes:

- `audit/`
- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)
- runtime memory logs
- local VSIX outputs

## Runtime Memory Notes

Generated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. These are intentionally excluded from source control to keep commits clean.

## Validation And Best Practices Docs

- Validation matrix report:
  - `docs/VALIDATION_REPORT.md`
  - `docs/VALIDATION_REPORT.json`
- Webview/performance/security implementation checklist:
  - `docs/BEST_PRACTICES.md`
```

## Tool Execution
Command: read README.md

```text
# NEXCODE-KIBOKO

Local-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.

## What You Get

- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.
- Copilot-style sidebar UX with session history, per-session model/provider/mode controls, and settings drawer.
- Provider connection badge with live health checks and latency display.
- Automatic model suggestions from Ollama/OpenAI-compatible `/models` endpoints.
- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.
- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.
- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.
- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.

## Repository Layout

- `extension/`: VS Code extension.
- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.
- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.
- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.
- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).
- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js `>=18`
- npm `>=9`
- VS Code `>=1.95`
- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.

## Quick Start

1. Install dependencies:
   - `npm install`
2. Build all packages:
   - `npm run build`
3. Optional model pull for local inference:
   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\tools\setup-ollama.ps1`
4. Launch extension host:
   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.
   - Press `F5`.
5. Open the sidebar:
   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.

## Recommended Ollama Model

The sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:

- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`

You can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.

## Chat Command Surface

- Standard prompt:
  - `Build auth middleware with tests`
- Local code search:
  - `/tool search orchestrator`
- Online search (Tavily + fallback):
  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`
- Terminal execution:
  - `/tool terminal npm run test`
- Git and tests:
  - `/tool git-status`
  - `/tool git-diff`
  - `/tool git-branch`
  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`
- Read file:
  - `/tool read README.md`
- Propose code edit:
  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`

## Approval And Safety Flow

- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.
- Every proposal supports:
  - `Preview Diff`
  - `Apply Edit`
  - `Reject`
- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.
- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.

## Extension Settings

All settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:

- `defaultProvider`
- `defaultModel`
- `defaultMode`
- `ollamaBaseUrl`
- `openAIBaseUrl`
- `openAIApiKey`
- `tavilyApiKey`
- `allowToolCommands`
- `requireTerminalApproval`

## Build, Test, Package

- Build:
  - `npm run build`
- Type/lint checks:
  - `npm run lint`
- Tests:
  - `npm run test`
- Full model and tools validation matrix:
  - `npm run validate:models`
- Raw VSIX package:
  - `npm run package:vsix`

## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump

Use the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:

- Package and install:
  - `npm run extension:release`
- Package only:
  - `npm run extension:package`
- Bump only:
  - `npm run extension:bump`

You can choose version increment type:

- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`
- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`

## Maintenance And Cleanup

- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:
  - `npm run clean`
- Run full local checks:
  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\tools\run-local-checks.ps1`

Ignored/generated content includes:

- `audit/`
- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)
- runtime memory logs
- local VSIX outputs

## Runtime Memory Notes

Generated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. These are intentionally excluded from source control to keep commits clean.

## Validation And Best Practices Docs

- Validation matrix report:
  - `docs/VALIDATION_REPORT.md`
  - `docs/VALIDATION_REPORT.json`
- Webview/performance/security implementation checklist:
  - `docs/BEST_PRACTICES.md`
```


You are leading a production migration for an ecommerce checkout API. Provide an actionable implementation plan with reliability controls, idempotency strategy, observability, rollback strategy, and high-value test plan.

## Planner
1. **Summary**: Migrate the ecommerce checkout API to production with reliability controls, idempotency strategy, observability, rollback strategy, and a high-value test plan.
2. **Steps**:
   - **Step 1: Assess Current Environment**
     - Files/components affected: `agent-core/`, `providers/`
     - Acceptance criteria: Document the current API architecture, dependencies, and any known issues.
   
   - **Step 2: Define Migration Strategy**
     - Files/components affected: `README.md`, `tools/migration-strategy.md`
     - Acceptance criteria: A detailed migration plan that includes:
       - The source code to be migrated (`agent-core/` and `providers/`)
       - Any required dependencies or external services (e.g., database, load balancer)
       - A rollback strategy in case of issues during the migration process
     - **Step 3: Implement Reliability Controls**
       - Files/components affected: `tools/`
       - Acceptance criteria: Implement logging and monitoring mechanisms to track API usage and detect any errors or failures. Use a tool like Prometheus, Grafana, or ELK stack for observability.
   
   - **Step 4: Ensure Idempotency**
     - Files/components affected: `tools/`

2. **Step 2: Define Migration Strategy**

5. **Step 5: Set Up Observability**
   - Continue implementing logging and monitoring mechanisms to track API usage and detect any errors or failures. Use Prometheus, Grafana, or ELK stack for observability.
6. **Step 6: Plan Rollback Strategy**
   - Implement a rollback strategy in case of issues during the migration process by creating a backup copy of the source code (`agent-core/`), restoring from version control, or using Terraform for infrastructure as code if required.

/tool read README.md

## Tool Execution
Command: read README.md

```text
# NEXCODE-KIBOKO

Local-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.

## What You Get

- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.
- Copilot-style sidebar UX with session history, per-session model/provider/mode controls, and settings drawer.
- Provider connection badge with live health checks and latency display.
- Automatic model suggestions from Ollama/OpenAI-compatible `/models` endpoints.
- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.
- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.
- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.
- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.

## Repository Layout

- `extension/`: VS Code extension.
- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.
- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.
- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.
- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).
- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js `>=18`
- npm `>=9`
- VS Code `>=1.95`
- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.

## Quick Start

1. Install dependencies:
   - `npm install`
2. Build all packages:
   - `npm run build`
3. Optional model pull for local inference:
   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\tools\setup-ollama.ps1`
4. Launch extension host:
   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.
   - Press `F5`.
5. Open the sidebar:
   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.

## Recommended Ollama Model

The sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:

- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`

You can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.

## Chat Command Surface

- Standard prompt:
  - `Build auth middleware with tests`
- Local code search:
  - `/tool search orchestrator`
- Online search (Tavily + fallback):
  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`
- Terminal execution:
  - `/tool terminal npm run test`
- Git and tests:
  - `/tool git-status`
  - `/tool git-diff`
  - `/tool git-branch`
  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`
- Read file:
  - `/tool read README.md`
- Propose code edit:
  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`

## Approval And Safety Flow

- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.
- Every proposal supports:
  - `Preview Diff`
  - `Apply Edit`
  - `Reject`
- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.
- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.

## Extension Settings

All settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:

- `defaultProvider`
- `defaultModel`
- `defaultMode`
- `ollamaBaseUrl`
- `openAIBaseUrl`
- `openAIApiKey`
- `tavilyApiKey`
- `allowToolCommands`
- `requireTerminalApproval`

## Build, Test, Package

- Build:
  - `npm run build`
- Type/lint checks:
  - `npm run lint`
- Tests:
  - `npm run test`
- Full model and tools validation matrix:
  - `npm run validate:models`
- Raw VSIX package:
  - `npm run package:vsix`

## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump

Use the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:

- Package and install:
  - `npm run extension:release`
- Package only:
  - `npm run extension:package`
- Bump only:
  - `npm run extension:bump`

You can choose version increment type:

- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`
- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`

## Maintenance And Cleanup

- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:
  - `npm run clean`
- Run full local checks:
  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\tools\run-local-checks.ps1`

Ignored/generated content includes:

- `audit/`
- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)
- runtime memory logs
- local VSIX outputs

## Runtime Memory Notes

Generated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. These are intentionally excluded from source control to keep commits clean.

## Validation And Best Practices Docs

- Validation matrix report:
  - `docs/VALIDATION_REPORT.md`
  - `docs/VALIDATION_REPORT.json`
- Webview/performance/security implementation checklist:
  - `docs/BEST_PRACTICES.md`

```

Install dependencies:\r\n   - `npm install`\r\n2. Build all packages:\r\n   - `npm run build`\r\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\r\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\tools\\setup-ollama.ps1`\r\n4. Launch extension host:\r\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\r\n   - Press `F5`.\r\n5. Open the sidebar:\r\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\r\n\r\n## Recommended Ollama Model\r\n\r\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\r\n\r\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\r\n\r\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\r\n\r\n## Chat Command Surface\r\n\r\n- Standard prompt:\r\n  - `Build auth middleware with tests`\r\n- Local code search:\r\n  - `/tool search orchestrator`\r\n- Online search (Tavily + fallback):\r\n  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`\r\n- Terminal execution:\r\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\r\n- Git and tests:\r\n  - `/tool git-status`\r\n  - `/tool git-diff`\r\n  - `/tool git-branch`\r\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\r\n- Read file:\r\n  - `/tool read README.md`\r\n- Propose code edit:\r\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\r\n\r\n## Approval And Safety Flow\r\n\r\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\r\n- Every proposal supports:\r\n  - `Preview Diff`\r\n  - `Apply Edit`\r\n  - `Reject`\r\n- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.\r\n- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.\r\n\r\n## Extension Settings\r\n\r\nAll settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:\r\n\r\n- `defaultProvider`\r\n- `defaultModel`\r\n- `defaultMode`\r\n- `ollamaBaseUrl`\r\n- `openAIBaseUrl`\r\n- `openAIApiKey`\r\n- `tavilyApiKey`\r\n- `allowToolCommands`\r\n- `requireTerminalApproval`\r\n\r\n## Build, Test, Package\r\n\r\n- Build:\r\n  - `npm run build`\r\n- Type/lint checks:\r\n  - `npm run lint`\r\n- Tests:\r\n  - `npm run test`\r\n- Raw VSIX package:\r\n  - `npm run package:vsix`\r\n\r\n## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump\r\n\r\nUse the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:\r\n\r\n- Package and install:\r\n  - `npm run extension:release`\r\n- Package only:\r\n  - `npm run extension:package`\r\n- Bump only:\r\n  - `npm run extension:bump`\r\n\r\nYou can choose version increment type:\r\n\r\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`\r\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`\r\n\r\n## Maintenance And Cleanup\r\n\r\n- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:\r\n  - `npm run clean`\r\n- Run full local checks:\r\n  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\tools\\run-local-checks.ps1`\r\n\r\nIgnored/generated content includes:\r\n\r\n- `audit/`\r\n- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)\r\n- runtime memory logs\r\n- local VSIX outputs\r\n\r\n## Runtime Memory Notes\r\n\r\nGenerated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. These are intentionally excluded from source control to keep commits clean.\r\n\n```","score":90,"acceptedEdits":0,"rejectedEdits":0,"metadata":{"mode":"auto","provider":"ollama","model":"kimi-k2.5:cloud","diagnosticsCount":0}}

modes.\\r\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\r\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\r\\n- Online web Code extension.\\r\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\r\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\r\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\r\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\r\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Prerequisites\\r\\n\\r\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\r\\n- npm `>=9`\\r\\n- VS Code Open the sidebar:\\r\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\r\\n\\r\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\r\\n\\r\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\r\\n\\r\\n## What You Get\\r\\n\\r\\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\\r\\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\\r\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\r\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\r\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\r\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Repository Layout\\r\\n\\r\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\r\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\r\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\r\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\r\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\r\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Prerequisites\\r\\n\\r\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\r\\n- npm `>=9`\\r\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\r\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Quick Start\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Install dependencies:\\r\\n   - `npm install`\\r\\n2. Build all packages:\\r\\n   - `npm run build`\\r\\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\\r\\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\tools\\\\setup-ollama.ps1`\\r\\n4. Launch extension host:\\r\\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\\r\\n   - Press `F5`.\\r\\n5. Open the sidebar:\\r\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\r\\n\\r\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\\r\\n\\r\\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Chat Command Surface\\r\\n\\r\\n- Standard prompt:\\r\\n  - `Build auth middleware with tests`\\r\\n- Local code search:\\r\\n  - `/tool search orchestrator`\\r\\n- Online search (Tavily + fallback):\\r\\n  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`\\r\\n- Terminal execution:\\r\\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\\r\\n- Git and tests:\\r\\n  - `/tool git-status`\\r\\n  - `/tool git-diff`\\r\\n  - `/tool git-branch`\\r\\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\\r\\n- Read file:\\r\\n  - `/tool read README.md`\\r\\n- Propose code edit:\\r\\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Approval And Safety Flow\\r\\n\\r\\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\\r\\n- Every proposal supports:\\r\\n  - `Preview Diff`\\r\\n  - `Apply Edit`\\r\\n  - `Reject`\\r\\n- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.\\r\\n- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Extension Settings\\r\\n\\r\\nAll settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `defaultProvider`\\r\\n- `defaultModel`\\r\\n- `defaultMode`\\r\\n- `ollamaBaseUrl`\\r\\n- `openAIBaseUrl`\\r\\n- `openAIApiKey`\\r\\n- `tavilyApiKey`\\r\\n- `allowToolCommands`\\r\\n- `requireTerminalApproval`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Build, Test, Package\\r\\n\\r\\n- Build:\\r\\n  - `npm run build`\\r\\n- Type/lint checks:\\r\\n  - `npm run lint`\\r\\n- Tests:\\r\\n  - `npm run test`\\r\\n- Raw VSIX package:\\r\\n  - `npm run package:vsix`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump\\r\\n\\r\\nUse the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:\\r\\n\\r\\n- Package and install:\\r\\n  - `npm run extension:release`\\r\\n- Package only:\\r\\n  - `npm run extension:package`\\r\\n- Bump only:\\r\\n  - `npm run extension:bump`\\r\\n\\r\\nYou can choose version increment type:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`\\r\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Maintenance And Cleanup\\r\\n\\r\\n- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:\\r\\n  - `npm run clean`\\r\\n- Run full local checks:\\r\\n  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\tools\\\\run-local-checks.ps1`\\r\\n\\r\\nIgnored/generated content includes:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `audit/`\\r\\n- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)\\r\\n- runtime memory logs\\r\\n- local VSIX outputs\\r\\n\\r\\n## Runtime Memory Notes\\r\\n\\r\\nGenerated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. ./memory/feedback-log.jsonl:16:{"timestamp":"2026-04-05T16:17:06.160Z","prompt":"/tool read README.md","response":"## Tool Execution\nCommand: read README.md\n\n```text\n# NEXCODE-KIBOKO\r\n\r\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\r\n\r\n## What You Get\r\n\r\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\r\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\r\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\r\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\r\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\r\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\r\n\r\n## Repository Layout\r\n\r\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\r\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\r\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\r\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\r\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\r\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\r\n\r\n## Prerequisites\r\n\r\n- Node.js `>=18`\r\n- npm `>=9`\r\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\r\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\r\n\r\n## Quick Start\r\n\r\n1. Install dependencies:\r\n   - `npm install`\r\n2. Build all packages:\r\n   - `npm run build`\r\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\r\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\tools\\setup-ollama.ps1`\r\n4. Launch extension host:\r\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\r\n   - Press `F5`.\r\n5. Open the sidebar:\r\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\r\n\r\n## Recommended Ollama Model\r\n\r\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\r\n\r\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\r\n\r\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\r\n\r\n## Chat Command Surface\r\n\r\n- Standard prompt:\r\n  - `Build auth middleware with tests`\r\n- Local code search:\r\n  - `/tool search orchestrator`\r\n- Online search (Tavily + fallback):\r\n  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`\r\n- Terminal execution:\r\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\r\n- Git and tests:\r\n  - `/tool git-status`\r\n  - `/tool git-diff`\r\n  - `/tool git-branch`\r\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\r\n- Read file:\r\n  - `/tool read README.md`\r\n- Propose code edit:\r\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\r\n\r\n## Approval And Safety Flow\r\n\r\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\r\n- Every proposal supports:\r\n  - `Preview Diff`\r\n  - `Apply Edit`\r\n  - `Reject`\r\n- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.\r\n- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.\r\n\r\n## Extension Settings\r\n\r\nAll settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:\r\n\r\n- `defaultProvider`\r\n- `defaultModel`\r\n- `defaultMode`\r\n- `ollamaBaseUrl`\r\n- `openAIBaseUrl`\r\n- `openAIApiKey`\r\n- `tavilyApiKey`\r\n- `allowToolCommands`\r\n- `requireTerminalApproval`\r\n\r\n## Build, Test, Package\r\n\r\n- Build:\r\n  - `npm run build`\r\n- Type/lint checks:\r\n  - `npm run lint`\r\n- Tests:\r\n  - `npm run test`\r\n- Raw VSIX package:\r\n  - `npm run package:vsix`\r\n\r\n## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump\r\n\r\nUse the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:\r\n\r\n- Package and install:\r\n  - `npm run extension:release`\r\n- Package only:\r\n  - `npm run extension:package`\r\n- Bump only:\r\n  - `npm run extension:bump`\r\n\r\nYou can choose version increment type:\r\n\r\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`\r\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`\r\n\r\n## Maintenance And Cleanup\r\n\r\n- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:\r\n  - `npm run clean`\r\n- Run full local checks:\r\n  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\tools\\run-local-checks.ps1`\r\n\r\nIgnored/generated content includes:\r\n\r\n- `audit/`\r\n- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)\r\n- runtime memory logs\r\n- local VSIX outputs\r\n\r\n## Runtime Memory Notes\r\n\r\nGenerated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. These are intentionally excluded from source control to keep commits clean.\r\n\n```","score":90,"acceptedEdits":0,"rejectedEdits":0,"metadata":{"mode":"auto","provider":"ollama","model":"qwen3-coder:480b-cloud","diagnosticsCount":0}}

read README.md\",\"response\":\"## Tool Execution\\nCommand: read README.md\\n\\n```text\\n# NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\r\\n\\r\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\r\\n\\r\\n## What You Get\\r\\n\\r\\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\\r\\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\\r\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\r\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\r\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\r\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Repository Layout\\r\\n\\r\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\r\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\r\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\r\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\r\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\r\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Prerequisites\\r\\n\\r\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\r\\n- npm `>=9`\\r\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\r\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Quick Start\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Install dependencies:\\r\\n   - `npm install`\\r\\n2. Build all packages:\\r\\n   - `npm run build`\\r\\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\\r\\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\tools\\\\setup-ollama.ps1`\\r\\n4. Launch extension host:\\r\\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\\r\\n   - Press `F5`.\\r\\n5. Open the sidebar:\\r\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\r\\n\\r\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\\r\\n\\r\\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Chat Command Surface\\r\\n\\r\\n- Standard prompt:\\r\\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\\r\\n- Git and tests:\\r\\n  - `/tool git-status`\\r\\n  - `/tool git-diff`\\r\\n  - `/tool git-branch`\\r\\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\\r\\n- Read file:\\r\\n  - `/tool read README.md`\\r\\n- Propose code edit:\\r\\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Approval And Safety Flow\\r\\n\\r\\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\\r\\n- Every proposal supports:\\r\\n  - README.md\\\\n\\\\n```text\\\\n# NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## What You Get\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\\\\r\\\\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\\\\r\\\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\\\r\\\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\\\r\\\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\\\r\\\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Repository Layout\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\\\r\\\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\\\r\\\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\\\r\\\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\\\r\\\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\\\r\\\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Prerequisites\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\\\r\\\\n- npm `>=9`\\\\r\\\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\\\r\\\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Quick Start\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n1. Install dependencies:\\\\r\\\\n   - `npm install`\\\\r\\\\n2. Build all packages:\\\\r\\\\n   - `npm run build`\\\\r\\\\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\\\\r\\\\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\\\\\tools\\\\\\\\setup-ollama.ps1`\\\\r\\\\n4. Launch extension host:\\\\r\\\\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\\\\r\\\\n   - Press `F5`.\\\\r\\\\n5. Open the sidebar:\\\\r\\\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Chat Command Surface\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Standard prompt:\\\\r\\\\n  - `Build auth middleware with tests`\\\\r\\\\n- Local code search:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool search orchestrator`\\\\r\\\\n- Online search (Tavily + fallback):\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`\\\\r\\\\n- Terminal execution:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\\\\r\\\\n- Git and tests:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-status`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-diff`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-branch`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\\\\r\\\\n- Read file:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool read README.md`\\\\r\\\\n- Propose code edit:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Approval And Safety Flow\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\\\\r\\\\n- Every proposal supports:\\\\r\\\\n  - `Preview Diff`\\\\r\\\\n  - `Apply Edit`\\\\r\\\\n  - `Reject`\\\\r\\\\n- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.\\\\r\\\\n- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Extension Settings\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nAll settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultProvider`\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultModel`\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultMode`\\\\r\\\\n- `ollamaBaseUrl`\\\\r\\\\n- `openAIBaseUrl`\\\\r\\\\n- `openAIApiKey`\\\\r\\\\n- `tavilyApiKey`\\\\r\\\\n- `allowToolCommands`\\\\r\\\\n- `requireTerminalApproval`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Build, Test, Package\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Build:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run build`\\\\r\\\\n- Type/lint checks:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run lint`\\\\r\\\\n- Tests:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run test`\\\\r\\\\n- Raw VSIX package:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run package:vsix`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nUse the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Package and install:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:release`\\\\r\\\\n- Package only:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:package`\\\\r\\\\n- Bump only:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:bump`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nYou can choose version increment type:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`\\\\r\\\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Maintenance And Cleanup\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:\\\\r\\\\n  - NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\r\\n\\r\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\r\\n\\r\\n## What You Get\\r\\n\\r\\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\\r\\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\\r\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\r\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\r\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\r\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Repository Layout\\r\\n\\r\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\r\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\r\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\r\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\r\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\r\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Prerequisites\\r\\n\\r\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\r\\n- npm `>=9`\\r\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\r\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Quick Start\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Install dependencies:\\r\\n   - `npm install`\\r\\n2. Build all packages:\\r\\n   - `npm run build`\\r\\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\\r\\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\tools\\\\setup-ollama.ps1`\\r\\n4. Launch extension host:\\r\\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\\r\\n   - Press `F5`.\\r\\n5. Open the sidebar:\\r\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\r\\n\\r\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\\r\\n\\r\\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Chat Command Surface\\r\\n\\r\\n- Standard prompt:\\r\\n  - `Build auth middleware with tests`\\r\\n- Local code search:\\r\\n  - `/tool search orchestrator`\\r\\n- Online search (Tavily + fallback):\\r\\n  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`\\r\\n- Terminal execution:\\r\\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\\r\\n- Git and tests:\\r\\n  - `/tool git-status`\\r\\n  - `/tool git-diff`\\r\\n  - `/tool git-branch`\\r\\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\\r\\n- Read file:\\r\\n  - `/tool read README.md`\\r\\n- Propose code edit:\\r\\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Approval And Safety Flow\\r\\n\\r\\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\\r\\n- Every proposal supports:\\r\\n  - `Preview Diff`\\r\\n  - `Apply Edit`\\r\\n  - `Reject`\\r\\n- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.\\r\\n- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Extension Settings\\r\\n\\r\\nAll settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `defaultProvider`\\r\\n- `defaultModel`\\r\\n- `defaultMode`\\r\\n- `ollamaBaseUrl`\\r\\n- `openAIBaseUrl`\\r\\n- `openAIApiKey`\\r\\n- `tavilyApiKey`\\r\\n- `allowToolCommands`\\r\\n- `requireTerminalApproval`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Build, Test, Package\\r\\n\\r\\n- Build:\\r\\n  - `npm run build`\\r\\n- Type/lint checks:\\r\\n  - `npm run lint`\\r\\n- Tests:\\r\\n  - `npm run test`\\r\\n- Raw VSIX package:\\r\\n  - `npm run package:vsix`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump\\r\\n\\r\\nUse the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:\\r\\n\\r\\n- Package and install:\\r\\n  - `npm run extension:release`\\r\\n- Package only:\\r\\n  - `npm run extension:package`\\r\\n- Bump only:\\r\\n  - `npm run extension:bump`\\r\\n\\r\\nYou can choose version increment type:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`\\r\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Maintenance And Cleanup\\r\\n\\r\\n- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:\\r\\n  - `npm run clean`\\r\\n- Run full local checks:\\r\\n  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\tools\\\\run-local-checks.ps1`\\r\\n\\r\\nIgnored/generated content includes:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `audit/`\\r\\n- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)\\r\\n- runtime memory logs\\r\\n- local VSIX outputs\\r\\n\\r\\n## Runtime Memory Notes\\r\\n\\r\\nGenerated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. These are intentionally excluded from source control to keep commits clean.\\r\\n\\n```\",\n./memory/long-term-memory.json:160:    \"text\": \"/tool search orchestrator\\n\\n## Tool README.md\\\\n\\\\n```text\\\\n# NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## What You Get\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\\\\r\\\\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\\\\r\\\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\\\r\\\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\\\r\\\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\\\r\\\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Repository Layout\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\\\r\\\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\\\r\\\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\\\r\\\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\\\r\\\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\\\r\\\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Prerequisites\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\\\r\\\\n- npm `>=9`\\\\r\\\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\\\r\\\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Quick Start\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n1. Install dependencies:\\\\r\\\\n   - `npm install`\\\\r\\\\n2. Build all packages:\\\\r\\\\n   - `npm run build`\\\\r\\\\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\\\\r\\\\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\\\\\tools\\\\\\\\setup-ollama.ps1`\\\\r\\\\n4. Launch extension host:\\\\r\\\\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\\\\r\\\\n   - Press `F5`.\\\\r\\\\n5. Open the sidebar:\\\\r\\\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Chat Command Surface\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Standard prompt:\\\\r\\\\n  - `Build auth middleware with tests`\\\\r\\\\n- Local code search:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool search orchestrator`\\\\r\\\\n- Online search (Tavily + fallback):\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`\\\\r\\\\n- Terminal execution:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\\\\r\\\\n- Git and tests:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-status`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-diff`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-branch`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\\\\r\\\\n- Read file:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool read README.md`\\\\r\\\\n- Propose code edit:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Approval And Safety Flow\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\\\\r\\\\n- Every proposal supports:\\\\r\\\\n  - `Preview Diff`\\\\r\\\\n  - `Apply Edit`\\\\r\\\\n  - `Reject`\\\\r\\\\n- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.\\\\r\\\\n- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Extension Settings\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nAll settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultProvider`\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultModel`\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultMode`\\\\r\\\\n- `ollamaBaseUrl`\\\\r\\\\n- `openAIBaseUrl`\\\\r\\\\n- `openAIApiKey`\\\\r\\\\n- `tavilyApiKey`\\\\r\\\\n- `allowToolCommands`\\\\r\\\\n- `requireTerminalApproval`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Build, Test, Package\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Build:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run build`\\\\r\\\\n- Type/lint checks:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run lint`\\\\r\\\\n- Tests:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run test`\\\\r\\\\n- Raw VSIX package:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run package:vsix`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nUse the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Package and install:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:release`\\\\r\\\\n- Package only:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:package`\\\\r\\\\n- Bump only:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:bump`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nYou can choose version increment type:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`\\\\r\\\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Maintenance And Cleanup\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:\\\\r\\\\n  - \"text\": \"/tool read README.md\\n\\n## Tool Execution\\nCommand: read README.md\\n\\n```text\\n# NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\r\\n\\r\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\r\\n\\r\\n## What You Get\\r\\n\\r\\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\\r\\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\\r\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\r\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\r\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\r\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Repository Layout\\r\\n\\r\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\r\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\r\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\r\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\r\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\r\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Prerequisites\\r\\n\\r\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\r\\n- npm `>=9`\\r\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\r\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Quick Start\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Install dependencies:\\r\\n   - `npm install`\\r\\n2. Build all packages:\\r\\n   - `npm run build`\\r\\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\\r\\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\tools\\\\setup-ollama.ps1`\\r\\n4. Launch extension host:\\r\\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\\r\\n   - Press `F5`.\\r\\n5. Open the sidebar:\\r\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\r\\n\\r\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\\r\\n\\r\\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Chat Command Surface\\r\\n\\r\\n- Standard prompt:\\r\\n  - `Build auth middleware with tests`\\r\\n- Local code search:\\r\\n  - `/tool search orchestrator`\\r\\n- Online search (Tavily + fallback):\\r\\n  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`\\r\\n- Terminal execution:\\r\\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\\r\\n- Git and tests:\\r\\n  - `/tool git-status`\\r\\n  - `/tool git-diff`\\r\\n  - `/tool git-branch`\\r\\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\\r\\n- Read file:\\r\\n  - `/tool read README.md`\\r\\n- Propose code edit:\\r\\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Approval And Safety Flow\\r\\n\\r\\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\\r\\n- Every proposal supports:\\r\\n  - `Preview Diff`\\r\\n  - `Apply Edit`\\r\\n  - `Reject`\\r\\n- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.\\r\\n- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Extension Settings\\r\\n\\r\\nAll settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `defaultProvider`\\r\\n- `defaultModel`\\r\\n- `defaultMode`\\r\\n- `ollamaBaseUrl`\\r\\n- `openAIBaseUrl`\\r\\n- `openAIApiKey`\\r\\n- `tavilyApiKey`\\r\\n- `allowToolCommands`\\r\\n- `requireTerminalApproval`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Build, Test, Package\\r\\n\\r\\n- Build:\\r\\n  - `npm run build`\\r\\n- Type/lint checks:\\r\\n  - `npm run lint`\\r\\n- Tests:\\r\\n  - `npm run test`\\r\\n- Raw VSIX package:\\r\\n  - `npm run package:vsix`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump\\r\\n\\r\\nUse the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:\\r\\n\\r\\n- Package and install:\\r\\n  - `npm run extension:release`\\r\\n- Package only:\\r\\n  - `npm run extension:package`\\r\\n- Bump only:\\r\\n  - `npm run extension:bump`\\r\\n\\r\\nYou can choose version increment type:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`\\r\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Maintenance And Cleanup\\r\\n\\r\\n- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:\\r\\n  - `npm run clean`\\r\\n- Run full local checks:\\r\\n  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\tools\\\\run-local-checks.ps1`\\r\\n\\r\\nIgnored/generated content includes:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `audit/`\\r\\n- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)\\r\\n- runtime memory logs\\r\\n- local VSIX outputs\\r\\n\\r\\n## Runtime Memory Notes\\r\\n\\r\\nGenerated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. These are intentionally excluded from source control to keep commits clean.\\r\\n\\n```\",\n./node_modules/@nexcode/agent-core/dist/index.d.ts:3:export * from ./memory/feedback-log.jsonl:25:{"timestamp":"2026-04-05T16:19:19.293Z","prompt":"/tool read README.md","response":"## Tool Execution\nCommand: read README.md\n\n```text\n# NEXCODE-KIBOKO\r\n\r\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\r\n\r\n## What You Get\r\n\r\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\r\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\r\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\r\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\r\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\r\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\r\n\r\n## Repository Layout\r\n\r\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\r\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\r\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\r\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\r\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\r\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\r\n\r\n## Prerequisites\r\n\r\n- Node.js `>=18`\r\n- npm `>=9`\r\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\r\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\r\n\r\n## Quick Start\r\n\r\n1. Install dependencies:\r\n   - `npm install`\r\n2. Build all packages:\r\n   - `npm run build`\r\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\r\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\tools\\setup-ollama.ps1`\r\n4. Launch extension host:\r\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\r\n   - Press `F5`.\r\n5. Open the sidebar:\r\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\r\n\r\n## Recommended Ollama Model\r\n\r\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\r\n\r\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\r\n\r\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\r\n\r\n## Chat Command Surface\r\n\r\n- Standard prompt:\r\n  - `Build auth middleware with tests`\r\n- Local code search:\r\n  - `/tool search orchestrator`\r\n- Online search (Tavily + fallback):\r\n  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`\r\n- Terminal execution:\r\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\r\n- Git and tests:\r\n  - `/tool git-status`\r\n  - `/tool git-diff`\r\n  - `/tool git-branch`\r\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\r\n- Read file:\r\n  - `/tool read README.md`\r\n- Propose code edit:\r\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\r\n\r\n## Approval And Safety Flow\r\n\r\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\r\n- Every proposal supports:\r\n  - `Preview Diff`\r\n  - `Apply Edit`\r\n  - `Reject`\r\n- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.\r\n- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.\r\n\r\n## Extension Settings\r\n\r\nAll settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:\r\n\r\n- `defaultProvider`\r\n- `defaultModel`\r\n- `defaultMode`\r\n- `ollamaBaseUrl`\r\n- `openAIBaseUrl`\r\n- `openAIApiKey`\r\n- `tavilyApiKey`\r\n- `allowToolCommands`\r\n- `requireTerminalApproval`\r\n\r\n## Build, Test, Package\r\n\r\n- Build:\r\n  - `npm run build`\r\n- Type/lint checks:\r\n  - `npm run lint`\r\n- Tests:\r\n  - `npm run test`\r\n- Raw VSIX package:\r\n  - `npm run package:vsix`\r\n\r\n## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump\r\n\r\nUse the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:\r\n\r\n- Package and install:\r\n  - `npm run extension:release`\r\n- Package only:\r\n  - `npm run extension:package`\r\n- Bump only:\r\n  - `npm run extension:bump`\r\n\r\nYou can choose version increment type:\r\n\r\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`\r\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`\r\n\r\n## Maintenance And Cleanup\r\n\r\n- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:\r\n  - `npm run clean`\r\n- Run full local checks:\r\n  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\tools\\run-local-checks.ps1`\r\n\r\nIgnored/generated content includes:\r\n\r\n- `audit/`\r\n- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)\r\n- runtime memory logs\r\n- local VSIX outputs\r\n\r\n## Runtime Memory Notes\r\n\r\nGenerated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. These are intentionally excluded from source control to keep commits clean.\r\n\n```","score":90,"acceptedEdits":0,"rejectedEdits":0,"metadata":{"mode":"auto","provider":"ollama","model":"qwen2.5-coder:7b","diagnosticsCount":0}}

read README.md\",\"response\":\"## Tool Execution\\nCommand: read README.md\\n\\n```text\\n# NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\r\\n\\r\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\r\\n\\r\\n## What You Get\\r\\n\\r\\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\\r\\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\\r\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\r\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\r\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\r\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Repository Layout\\r\\n\\r\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\r\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\r\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\r\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\r\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\r\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Prerequisites\\r\\n\\r\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\r\\n- npm `>=9`\\r\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\r\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Quick Start\\r\\n\\r\\n1. Install dependencies:\\r\\n   - `npm install`\\r\\n2. Build all packages:\\r\\n   - `npm run build`\\r\\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\\r\\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\tools\\\\setup-ollama.ps1`\\r\\n4. Launch extension host:\\r\\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\\r\\n   - Press `F5`.\\r\\n5. Open the sidebar:\\r\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\r\\n\\r\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\\r\\n\\r\\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\\r\\n\\r\\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Chat Command Surface\\r\\n\\r\\n- Standard prompt:\\r\\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\\r\\n- Git and tests:\\r\\n  - `/tool git-status`\\r\\n  - `/tool git-diff`\\r\\n  - `/tool git-branch`\\r\\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\\r\\n- Read file:\\r\\n  - `/tool read README.md`\\r\\n- Propose code edit:\\r\\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\\r\\n\\r\\n## Approval And Safety Flow\\r\\n\\r\\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\\r\\n- Every proposal supports:\\r\\n  - README.md\\\\n\\\\n```text\\\\n# NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## What You Get\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\\\\r\\\\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\\\\r\\\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\\\r\\\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\\\r\\\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\\\r\\\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Repository Layout\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\\\r\\\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\\\r\\\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\\\r\\\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\\\r\\\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\\\r\\\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Prerequisites\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\\\r\\\\n- npm `>=9`\\\\r\\\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\\\r\\\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Quick Start\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n1. Install dependencies:\\\\r\\\\n   - `npm install`\\\\r\\\\n2. Build all packages:\\\\r\\\\n   - `npm run build`\\\\r\\\\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\\\\r\\\\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\\\\\tools\\\\\\\\setup-ollama.ps1`\\\\r\\\\n4. Launch extension host:\\\\r\\\\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\\\\r\\\\n   - Press `F5`.\\\\r\\\\n5. Open the sidebar:\\\\r\\\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Chat Command Surface\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Standard prompt:\\\\r\\\\n  - `Build auth middleware with tests`\\\\r\\\\n- Local code search:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool search orchestrator`\\\\r\\\\n- Online search (Tavily + fallback):\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`\\\\r\\\\n- Terminal execution:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\\\\r\\\\n- Git and tests:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-status`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-diff`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-branch`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\\\\r\\\\n- Read file:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool read README.md`\\\\r\\\\n- Propose code edit:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Approval And Safety Flow\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\\\\r\\\\n- Every proposal supports:\\\\r\\\\n  - `Preview Diff`\\\\r\\\\n  - `Apply Edit`\\\\r\\\\n  - `Reject`\\\\r\\\\n- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.\\\\r\\\\n- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Extension Settings\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nAll settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultProvider`\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultModel`\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultMode`\\\\r\\\\n- `ollamaBaseUrl`\\\\r\\\\n- `openAIBaseUrl`\\\\r\\\\n- `openAIApiKey`\\\\r\\\\n- `tavilyApiKey`\\\\r\\\\n- `allowToolCommands`\\\\r\\\\n- `requireTerminalApproval`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Build, Test, Package\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Build:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run build`\\\\r\\\\n- Type/lint checks:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run lint`\\\\r\\\\n- Tests:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run test`\\\\r\\\\n- Raw VSIX package:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run package:vsix`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nUse the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Package and install:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:release`\\\\r\\\\n- Package only:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:package`\\\\r\\\\n- Bump only:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:bump`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nYou can choose version increment type:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`\\\\r\\\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Maintenance And Cleanup\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:\\\\r\\\\n  - modes.\\r\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\r\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\r\\n- Online web Code extension.\\r\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\r\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\r\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\r\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\r\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Prerequisites\\r\\n\\r\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\r\\n- npm `>=9`\\r\\n- VS Code Launch extension host:\\r\\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\\r\\n   - Press `F5`.\\r\\n5. Open the sidebar:\\r\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\r\\n\\r\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## What You Get\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- VS search.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Repository Layout\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools `>=18`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- npm `>=9`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Quick Start\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n1. Open the sidebar:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. Tool Execution\\\\\\\\nCommand: read README.md\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```text\\\\\\\\n# NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## What You Get\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Repository Layout\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Prerequisites\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- npm `>=9`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Quick Start\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n1. Install dependencies:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n   - `npm install`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n2. Build all packages:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n   - `npm run build`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tools\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\setup-ollama.ps1`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n4. Launch extension host:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n   - Press `F5`.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n5. Open the sidebar:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Chat Command Surface\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Standard prompt:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `Build auth middleware with tests`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Local code search:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `/tool search orchestrator`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Online search (Tavily + fallback):\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Terminal execution:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Git and tests:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `/tool git-status`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `/tool git-diff`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `/tool git-branch`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Read file:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `/tool read README.md`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Propose code edit:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Approval And Safety Flow\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Every proposal supports:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `Preview Diff`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `Apply Edit`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `Reject`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Extension Settings\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nAll settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `defaultProvider`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `defaultModel`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `defaultMode`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `ollamaBaseUrl`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `openAIBaseUrl`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `openAIApiKey`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `tavilyApiKey`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `allowToolCommands`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `requireTerminalApproval`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Build, Test, Package\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Build:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `npm run build`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Type/lint checks:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `npm run lint`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Tests:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `npm run test`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Raw VSIX package:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `npm run package:vsix`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nUse the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Package and install:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `npm run extension:release`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Package only:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `npm run extension:package`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Bump only:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `npm run extension:bump`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nYou can choose version increment type:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Maintenance And Cleanup\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `npm run clean`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Run full local checks:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\tools\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\run-local-checks.ps1`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nIgnored/generated content includes:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `audit/`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- runtime memory logs\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- local VSIX outputs\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Runtime Memory Notes\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nGenerated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. README.md\\\\n\\\\n```text\\\\n# NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## What You Get\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.\\\\r\\\\n- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.\\\\r\\\\n- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.\\\\r\\\\n- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.\\\\r\\\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\\\r\\\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Repository Layout\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\\\r\\\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.\\\\r\\\\n- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.\\\\r\\\\n- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.\\\\r\\\\n- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).\\\\r\\\\n- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Prerequisites\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Node.js `>=18`\\\\r\\\\n- npm `>=9`\\\\r\\\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\\\r\\\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Quick Start\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n1. Install dependencies:\\\\r\\\\n   - `npm install`\\\\r\\\\n2. Build all packages:\\\\r\\\\n   - `npm run build`\\\\r\\\\n3. Optional model pull for local inference:\\\\r\\\\n   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\\\\\tools\\\\\\\\setup-ollama.ps1`\\\\r\\\\n4. Launch extension host:\\\\r\\\\n   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.\\\\r\\\\n   - Press `F5`.\\\\r\\\\n5. Open the sidebar:\\\\r\\\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nYou can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Chat Command Surface\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Standard prompt:\\\\r\\\\n  - `Build auth middleware with tests`\\\\r\\\\n- Local code search:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool search orchestrator`\\\\r\\\\n- Online search (Tavily + fallback):\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`\\\\r\\\\n- Terminal execution:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool terminal npm run test`\\\\r\\\\n- Git and tests:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-status`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-diff`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool git-branch`\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`\\\\r\\\\n- Read file:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/tool read README.md`\\\\r\\\\n- Propose code edit:\\\\r\\\\n  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Approval And Safety Flow\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.\\\\r\\\\n- Every proposal supports:\\\\r\\\\n  - `Preview Diff`\\\\r\\\\n  - `Apply Edit`\\\\r\\\\n  - `Reject`\\\\r\\\\n- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.\\\\r\\\\n- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Extension Settings\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nAll settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultProvider`\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultModel`\\\\r\\\\n- `defaultMode`\\\\r\\\\n- `ollamaBaseUrl`\\\\r\\\\n- `openAIBaseUrl`\\\\r\\\\n- `openAIApiKey`\\\\r\\\\n- `tavilyApiKey`\\\\r\\\\n- `allowToolCommands`\\\\r\\\\n- `requireTerminalApproval`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Build, Test, Package\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Build:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run build`\\\\r\\\\n- Type/lint checks:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run lint`\\\\r\\\\n- Tests:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run test`\\\\r\\\\n- Raw VSIX package:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run package:vsix`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nUse the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Package and install:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:release`\\\\r\\\\n- Package only:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:package`\\\\r\\\\n- Bump only:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run extension:bump`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nYou can choose version increment type:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`\\\\r\\\\n- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Maintenance And Cleanup\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:\\\\r\\\\n  - `npm run clean`\\\\r\\\\n- Run full local checks:\\\\r\\\\n  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\\\\\\\tools\\\\\\\\run-local-checks.ps1`\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nIgnored/generated content includes:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `audit/`\\\\r\\\\n- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)\\\\r\\\\n- runtime memory logs\\\\r\\\\n- local VSIX outputs\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n## Runtime Memory Notes\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nGenerated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. NEXCODE-KIBOKO\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nLocal-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## What You Get\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- VS search.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Repository Layout\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `extension/`: VS Code extension.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools `>=18`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- npm `>=9`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- VS Code `>=1.95`\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Quick Start\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n1. Open the sidebar:\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n## Recommended Ollama Model\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\r\\\\\\\\nThe sidebar model field is editable. ## Tool Execution
Command: read README.md

```text
# NEXCODE-KIBOKO

Local-first, multi-agent AI coding assistant for VS Code.

## What You Get

- VS Code sidebar extension with chat, streaming, attachments, and controlled edit approvals.
- Copilot-style sidebar UX with session history, per-session model/provider/mode controls, and settings drawer.
- Provider connection badge with live health checks and latency display.
- Automatic model suggestions from Ollama/OpenAI-compatible `/models` endpoints.
- Multi-agent core with `auto`, `planner`, `coder`, `reviewer`, `qa`, and `security` modes.
- Provider routing for local Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
- Tooling layer for filesystem, terminal, git, tests, local code search, and online web search.
- Online web search through Tavily with DuckDuckGo and Wikipedia fallbacks.
- Persistent memory and feedback logs for iterative prompt refinement.

## Repository Layout

- `extension/`: VS Code extension.
- `agent-core/`: orchestration and tools runtime.
- `prompts/`: editable system prompts.
- `providers/`: provider templates and examples.
- `memory/`: runtime memory store (generated files are ignored).
- `tools/`: setup, cleanup, packaging, and release scripts.

## Prerequisites

- Node.js `>=18`
- npm `>=9`
- VS Code `>=1.95`
- Ollama installed and running for local model usage.

## Quick Start

1. Install dependencies:
   - `npm install`
2. Build all packages:
   - `npm run build`
3. Optional model pull for local inference:
   - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\tools\setup-ollama.ps1`
4. Launch extension host:
   - Open `extension/` in VS Code.
   - Press `F5`.
5. Open the sidebar:
   - Run command `NEXCODE: Open Sidebar`.

## Recommended Ollama Model

The sidebar model field is editable. For advanced runs, set model to:

- `gpt-oss:120b-cloud`

You can still use lighter models such as `qwen2.5-coder:7b` for faster local iteration.

## Chat Command Surface

- Standard prompt:
  - `Build auth middleware with tests`
- Local code search:
  - `/tool search orchestrator`
- Online search (Tavily + fallback):
  - `/tool web-search OWASP API Security Top 10`
- Terminal execution:
  - `/tool terminal npm run test`
- Git and tests:
  - `/tool git-status`
  - `/tool git-diff`
  - `/tool git-branch`
  - `/tool test npm test -- --runInBand`
- Read file:
  - `/tool read README.md`
- Propose code edit:
  - `/edit agent-core/src/orchestrator.ts :: add retry around provider call`

## Approval And Safety Flow

- Edit proposals are never auto-applied.
- Every proposal supports:
  - `Preview Diff`
  - `Apply Edit`
  - `Reject`
- Terminal commands can require explicit confirmation from the sidebar.
- High-risk destructive command patterns are blocked in `agent-core` terminal tool policy.

## Extension Settings

All settings are under `nexcodeKiboko.*`:

- `defaultProvider`
- `defaultModel`
- `defaultMode`
- `ollamaBaseUrl`
- `openAIBaseUrl`
- `openAIApiKey`
- `tavilyApiKey`
- `allowToolCommands`
- `requireTerminalApproval`

## Build, Test, Package

- Build:
  - `npm run build`
- Type/lint checks:
  - `npm run lint`
- Tests:
  - `npm run test`
- Full model and tools validation matrix:
  - `npm run validate:models`
- Raw VSIX package:
  - `npm run package:vsix`

## Extension Install And Auto Version Bump

Use the release script to bump version, package, and install into VS Code:

- Package and install:
  - `npm run extension:release`
- Package only:
  - `npm run extension:package`
- Bump only:
  - `npm run extension:bump`

You can choose version increment type:

- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type patch`
- `node tools/extension-release.mjs --bump-type minor --no-install`

## Maintenance And Cleanup

- Clean generated simulation/artifact files:
  - `npm run clean`
- Run full local checks:
  - `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\tools\run-local-checks.ps1`

Ignored/generated content includes:

- `audit/`
- framework caches (`.next`, pytest cache, bytecode)
- runtime memory logs
- local VSIX outputs

## Runtime Memory Notes

Generated runtime memory files are written to `memory/` during use. These are intentionally excluded from source control to keep commits clean.

## Validation And Best Practices Docs

- Validation matrix report:
  - `docs/VALIDATION_REPORT.md`
  - `docs/VALIDATION_REPORT.json`
- Webview/performance/security implementation checklist:
  - `docs/BEST_PRACTICES.md`

```
